source.page_label: Key Dates | South Dakota Game, Fish & Parks
source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory game bird season data from SD GFP key dates page. South Dakota follows USFWS frameworks. Migratory Bird Certification ($5) required in addition to a valid hunting license for all migratory bird hunting.

species.name: Duck (Ducks & Mergansers)
species.season_raw: See zones below for zone-specific dates
species.hunting_units: Low Plains North, Low Plains Middle, Low Plains South, and High Plains (see sub-seasons)

species.name: Canada Goose
species.season_raw: Early Sep 1-30; then Unit 1 and Unit 2 (see sub-seasons)
species.hunting_units: Early Season (statewide), Unit 1, and Unit 2 (see sub-seasons)

species.name: Youth Waterfowl (Resident Only)
species.bag_limit: Same as regular duck/goose season limits

species.name: Crow
species.season_raw: Fall: Sep 1 - Oct 31; Spring: Mar 1 - Apr 30

licenses.name: Resident Small Game License
licenses.covers: All small game including migratory birds (does not include fishing)

licenses.name: Migratory Bird Certification
licenses.covers: Required in addition to a hunting license to hunt any migratory bird (ducks, geese, dove, snipe, crane, coot)

licenses.name: Federal Duck Stamp

purchase_urls.label: South Dakota Go Outdoors Sales Portal

key_notes.title: A Migratory Bird Certification ($5) is required in addition to a valid hunting license to hunt any migratory game bird in South Dakota (ducks, geese, dove, snipe, crane, coot).

sources.label: SD GFP — Licenses & Permits
sources.evidence: A Migratory Bird Certification ($5) is required for all migratory bird hunters in addition to a valid hunting license. Covers ducks, geese, dove, snipe, crane, and coot.

key_notes.title: A Federal Duck Stamp ($25) is required for all waterfowl hunters age 16 or older (ducks, geese, mergansers).

sources.label: USFWS — Federal Duck Stamp
sources.evidence: All waterfowl hunters 16 years of age or older must possess a valid Federal Duck Stamp.

key_notes.title: HIP Certification (Harvest Information Program) is required for all migratory bird hunters and is included when purchasing a hunting license in South Dakota.

sources.label: SD GFP — Hunting Information
sources.evidence: HIP certification is required for all migratory bird hunters and is obtained when purchasing a hunting license. There is no additional fee for HIP certification in South Dakota.

key_notes.title: South Dakota duck hunting has species-specific restrictions within the 6-bird daily bag: species limits apply per USFWS regulations.

sources.label: SD GFP — Waterfowl Regulations
sources.evidence: Daily bag limit for ducks is 6 in the aggregate, with species-specific restrictions including limits on mallards, pintails, canvasbacks, redheads, and scaup as established by USFWS.

key_notes.title: Youth Waterfowl Weekend (September 12-13, 2026) is open to resident youth hunters only, with the same bag limits as the regular season. Youth must be accompanied by a non-hunting adult 18+.

sources.label: SD GFP — Key Dates
sources.evidence: Youth Waterfowl Season is September 12-13, 2026, open to resident youth hunters only with same bag limits as regular waterfowl season.

key_notes.title: Sandhill Crane hunting requires a free Sandhill Crane Hunting Permit in addition to a valid hunting license and Migratory Bird Certification.

sources.label: SD GFP — Sandhill Crane Hunting
sources.evidence: A free Sandhill Crane Hunting Permit is required to hunt sandhill cranes in South Dakota, available through the SD GFP licensing system.

key_notes.title: Light geese (snow, blue, Ross's geese) have no daily or possession limit during the regular season. Conservation order measures may extend the season beyond January 8.

sources.label: SD GFP — Key Dates
sources.evidence: Light goose season runs September 26, 2026 through January 8, 2027 with no daily limit and no possession limit.
